 {"id":"bd-30o1y","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign --clear assignment removal logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for ntm assign --clear functionality.

## Test Cases

### Single Clear
- Clear assigned bead
- Verify assignment removed from store
- Verify file reservations released

### Batch Clear
- Clear multiple beads (comma-separated)
- Verify all cleared correctly
- Partial failure handling

### Clear by Pane
- Clear all assignments for pane 3
- Handle pane with no assignments

### Clear Failed
- Clear only failed assignments
- Leave working/completed untouched

### Error Handling
- Clear non-assigned bead -> error
- Clear completed bead without --force -> error
- Clear completed bead with --force -> success

### Edge Cases
- Clear already cleared assignment
- Clear during concurrent assignment

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-30ws","title":"Task: Implement SummarizeSession() function","description":"## Purpose
Generate concise, actionable summaries of agent session output at session end.

## Scope
- Implement SummarizeSession() in internal/summary/generator.go
- Extract key accomplishments, changes made, and pending work
- Parse structured output from agent sessions
- Support multiple summary formats (brief, detailed, handoff)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summaries capture all significant changes
- File changes are listed with context
- Pending work items are extracted
- Error summary included if applicable
- Token count reasonable for injection into next session

## Technical Notes
- Consider using LLM for summarization if available
- Fallback to pattern-based extraction
- Integrate with Agent Mail for thread summaries","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:00:21.180715869Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T00:56:40.940638964Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T00:56:40.940593108Z","close_reason":"Implemented SummarizeSession in internal/summary and wired into CLI","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30ws","depends_on_id":"bd-3dxk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:00:21.200265764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31e1f","title":"Feature: Robot Diagnose Command (--robot-diagnose)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-diagnose <session>` command that provides a single comprehensive
health check answering "what's wrong and how do I fix it?"

## Background

Currently diagnosing swarm health requires:
1. Loop through each pane
2. Capture and grep output for error patterns
3. Check process state
4. Interpret results
5. Decide on action

This is tedious and error-prone. A single diagnose command should do all of this.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
ntm --robot-diagnose myproject
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:15:00Z",
  "overall_health": "degraded",  // "healthy", "degraded", "critical"
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 16,
    "healthy": 12,
    "rate_limited": 2,
    "unresponsive": 1,
    "crashed": 1
  },
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16],
    "rate_limited": [4, 10],
    "unresponsive": [15],
    "crashed": [1]
  },
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "pane": 4,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "reset_time": "4pm ET",  // parsed from output if available
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 10,
      "status": "rate_limited",
      "action": "wait_or_caam",
      "reason": "Output contains 'You've hit your limit'",
      "auto_fixable": false
    },
    {
      "pane": 15,
      "status": "unresponsive",
      "action": "kill_restart",
      "reason": "No output for 312 seconds, process state: sleeping",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 15"
    },
    {
      "pane": 1,
      "status": "crashed",
      "action": "restart_cc",
      "reason": "Shell prompt visible, no agent process",
      "auto_fixable": true,
      "fix_command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 1"
    }
  ],
  "auto_fix_available": true,
  "auto_fix_command": "ntm --robot-diagnose myproject --fix"
}
```

## Health Classification Logic

| Status | Detection Criteria |
|--------|-------------------|
| healthy | Agent process running, recent output, no error patterns |
| rate_limited | Output contains rate limit patterns |
| unresponsive | No output for >5min AND process running |
| crashed | No agent process OR zombie process |
| unknown | Cannot determine (provide raw data) |

## Flags

- `--fix`: Automatically fix what can be fixed (crashed/unresponsive, not rate limits)
- `--json`: (default) JSON output
- `--brief`: Just summary, no per-pane details
- `--pane=N`: Diagnose specific pane only

## Why This Matters

Reduces "what's wrong?" from a 5-minute manual inspection to a 1-second command.
The recommendations with fix commands enable scripted recovery.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:16:03.415109604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.661480205Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:18:25.660292387Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-diagnose command with health classification, recommendations, and auto-fix support. Created internal/robot/diagnose.go and added flags to root.go.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31e1f","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:34:21.641865917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31e1f","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:16:03.445014389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31r17","title":"Feature: DCG guard integration for destructive commands","description":"## Overview
Use DCG as an external safety guard for dangerous shell commands initiated via NTM (and in robot workflows).

## Scope
- Provide a lightweight wrapper to preflight commands with DCG
- Surface DCG findings in robot output
- Keep behavior opt-in and safe-by-default

## Acceptance Criteria
- NTM can check a command via DCG when available
- Clear error when DCG not installed
- Robot output includes allow/deny + rationale","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Feature: Extend checkpoints with assignments/progress","description":"## Overview
Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkpoints remain readable even without new fields
- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled
- Restore flow surfaces assignment summary","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:12.436057119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:21.091234924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32ck","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:35:20.994759649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-332h","title":"Task: Implement file edit detection patterns","description":"## Purpose
Extend robot/patterns.go with patterns to detect file edits from agent output.

## Detection Patterns per Agent Type
var fileWritePatterns = map[AgentType][]string{
    AgentClaude: {
        'using the edit tool',
        'using the write tool',
        'Edited file:',
        'Created file:',
        'Writing to file:',
    },
    AgentCodex: {
        'writing to file',
        'edit.*file',
        'created file',
    },
    AgentGemini: {
        'Writing:',
        'Editing:',
        'Created:',
    },
}

## Path Extraction
func ExtractEditedFiles(output string, agentType AgentType) []string

Extract file paths from patterns like:
- 'Edited file: internal/cli/send.go'
- 'Writing to file /path/to/file.go'
- Tool use JSON with file_path field

## Acceptance Criteria
- Patterns detect 90%+ of file edits
- File paths extracted accurately
- Both relative and absolute paths handled
- False positives minimized

## Technical Notes
Use regexp.MustCompile for pattern matching
Test against actual agent output samples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:28.921638062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773414528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T10:01:31.773257222Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-332h","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:58:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
